name: Open Data Product Graphs
purpose: Graph reasoning, traversal, governance propagation, strategic intelligence, and AI-agent interaction across Open Data Product ecosystems.

description: URL of the ODPG schema used for validation.
Version of the ODPG specification.
Type of graph specification document. Use Graph.
Container for graph metadata, nodes, and edges.
Unique identifier of the graph.
Human-readable graph name using language-specific values.
Human-readable graph description using language-specific values.
Collection of graph node objects.
Collection of directed graph edge objects.
Unique identifier of the node.
Type of graph entity represented by the node.
Path or URI to the referenced specification or resource.
Source node identifier.
Target node identifier.
Relationship type connecting two nodes.
Confidence level of the relationship. Use high, medium, or low.
A data product defined using ODPS or compatible structures.
retrievalUse: Select when the graph references a data product without embedding full product metadata.

description: A business, analytical, operational, or AI-related use case.
retrievalUse: Select when modeling a demand, scenario, workflow need, or business application.

description: A strategic business objective or organizational goal.
retrievalUse: Select when modeling desired business outcomes or strategic goals.

description: A measurable business or operational indicator.
retrievalUse: Select when modeling a metric that measures outcomes, objectives, or product contribution.

description: A business, organizational, technical, or data domain.
retrievalUse: Select when grouping ownership, capability, or subject-area context.

description: A structured or unstructured dataset.
retrievalUse: Select when a dataset needs to be represented separately from a data product.

description: A service interface exposed or consumed by a data product, agent, system, or workflow.
retrievalUse: Select when modeling technical access, interface exposure, or service consumption.

description: A governance, compliance, security, or quality policy.
retrievalUse: Select when modeling governance requirements or controls.

description: A business or technical workflow.
retrievalUse: Select when modeling process execution, orchestration, or operational flows.

description: An AI agent or automation actor.
retrievalUse: Select when modeling AI-agent interaction, automation, or graph-native reasoning.

description: A business or technical capability.
retrievalUse: Select when modeling reusable organizational or technical capabilities.

description: An inferred or declared strategic opportunity.
retrievalUse: Select when graph relationships indicate unmet needs, optimizations, gaps, or emerging value.

description: A node uses another node as part of execution or operation.
A node supports a business objective.
A node contributes toward an outcome or objective.
A KPI measures an objective or outcome.
A node tracks or provides KPI-related information.
A node depends on another node.
A node produces data, outputs, or services.
A node consumes data, APIs, or outputs.
A node is governed by a policy or control.
A node is owned by a person, team, organization, or domain.
A node aligns strategically or semantically with another node.
A generic semantic relationship.
A node impacts another node.
A node originates from another node.
A node exposes an API or interface.
A node monitors another node.
A node identifies an opportunity or condition.
Relationship is explicitly declared or confirmed.
agentUse: Prefer for source-backed or owner-confirmed relationships.

description: Relationship is partially validated or inferred with moderate certainty.
agentUse: Use for plausible inferred relationships with some evidence.

description: Relationship is inferred and requires additional validation.
agentUse: Use when suggesting relationships that need review.

description: Validates ODPG documents against the official schema and verifies structural consistency, node integrity, edge validity, confidence values, and interoperability requirements.
Enables traversal across interconnected graph entities using semantic, strategic, operational, and governance relationships.
Analyzes graph relationships to identify organizational gaps, overlapping initiatives, unsupported objectives, and emerging strategic opportunities.
Enables AI agents and automation systems to interact with ODPG ecosystems through graph-native semantic interfaces and governance-aware traversal mechanisms.
Enables organizations to connect multiple ODPG ecosystems into federated graph environments for cross-domain interoperability, distributed governance, and enterprise-scale graph reasoning.
Python command-line script for ODPG graph validation, node integrity checks, edge integrity checks, confidence validation, and core semantic warnings.
Python command-line script for summarizing ODPG graph metadata, node counts, edge counts, node types, edge types, and confidence values.
Python command-line script for discovering ODPG relationship paths from a node, with optional relationship filtering and reverse traversal.
Python command-line script for ODPG strategic and governance analysis, including orphan KPI, unsupported objective, ungoverned asset, and low-confidence relationship detection.
Python command-line script for extracting trusted graph context around a focus node for AI-agent use.
Python generator that converts an ODPG YAML graph into a standalone HTML Graph Explorer.
An AI agent uses a data product and an API to retrieve trusted graph context or perform governed analysis.
A data product contributes to a measurable business objective and tracks a KPI.
A data product or API is governed by a policy, enabling governance-aware traversal and impact analysis.
